hero.title: Every span, verdict and dollar, kept on your machine
hero.sub: NullWatch ingests execution traces and eval results from NullClaw, stores them as local JSONL files, and serves run summaries — latency, errors, tokens, cost — over a JSON HTTP API and CLI. Headless by design; NullHub renders the UI.

alts.label: Build from source (Zig 0.16.0)
alts.code:
git clone https://github.com/nullclaw/nullwatch
cd nullwatch
zig build
zig build run -- serve

quickstart:
Seed a deterministic demo dataset — no API keys, no running agent — then read it back.

```bash
# seed demo runs: a passing review, a failed tool call, a retry
zig build run -- demo-seed

# start the JSON HTTP API on 127.0.0.1:7710
zig build run -- serve

# list runs and inspect the failure
zig build run -- runs --limit 20
zig build run -- run demo-tool-failure

# same data over HTTP
curl http://127.0.0.1:7710/v1/runs?limit=20
```

features.title: Span ingest for agents
features.body: Records each timed unit inside a run — model calls, tool invocations, memory lookups, retries — with tokens, cost and status attached.

features.title: Evals as first-class data
features.body: Scored assertions attach to runs: scorer, score, verdict, dataset. Regression gates and rubric checks live next to the traces they judge.

features.title: Run-level summaries
features.body: Each run gets computed totals: duration, error count, token usage, cost, pass/fail counts and an overall verdict.

features.title: A tiny OTLP sink
features.body: Accepts OTLP/HTTP JSON traces on /v1/traces. Point NullClaw's diagnostics OTLP endpoint at it and spans map into runs automatically.

features.title: Local JSONL storage
features.body: Everything lands under ~/.nullwatch/data as plain JSONL. Storage is designed to swap for SQLite later without changing the API contract.

features.title: Demo data built in
features.body: demo-seed creates a deterministic, idempotent dataset — runs, spans, evals, costs, failures — without API keys or a hosted service.

note: Pre-1.0 with one release so far; the README calls it a bootstrap implementation, and the repo has no license file yet.
